target_market: Adults injured in auto, truck, motorcycle, pedestrian, premises-liability, or wrongful-death incidents across Metro Atlanta (Atlanta, Sandy Springs, Dunwoody, Jonesboro, College Park, Forest Park, Lovejoy, Morrow, Riverdale, and Clayton/Cobb/Cherokee/DeKalb/Fulton/Gwinnett counties), who need a contingency-fee attorney and a free consultation. Secondary market: Metro Atlanta residents considering Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy.
positioning: The personal, hands-on alternative to the mega-firm 'settlement mill' competitors dominating Atlanta personal injury search results. Peter Bricks has been a member in good standing of the State Bar of Georgia since 2006 (19 years), handles cases personally rather than handing them to case managers, and has a documented track record including a $1,325,000 recovery and a $300,000 jury verdict. Free consultation, contingency-fee representation, no upfront cost to the client.
differentiators: 19 years licensed with the State Bar of Georgia (since 2006), Avvo 'Distinguished' rating with 22 reviews at 5.0 and a Client's Choice / Top Contributor distinction

Documented settlement track record: $1,325,000 (a case a prior attorney had under-settled), $300,000 jury verdict against a denied-causation defense, plus $100,000, $75,000, and a confidential result, all auto-collision cases
Personal, single-attorney attention rather than a high-volume case-manager model, directly addressing the impersonal feel of larger competing firms
Registered civil mediator through the Georgia Office of Dispute Resolution (GODR), an additional credibility signal not used by most competitors
Existing 'Lawyers and Lay People' podcast covering bankruptcy and personal injury topics, a content asset that can be repurposed for the new blog stream and social content
Handles pedestrian and hit-and-run claims (uninsured motorist coverage), a confirmed niche service most general competitors do not highlight
secondary_keywords: no win no fee lawyer Atlanta
Georgia personal injury statute of limitations
competitor_gaps: Bricks Law currently has zero dedicated case-type pages while John Foy & Associates, Atlanta Injury Law Group, Hammers Law Firm, Goldstein Hayes, and Gary Martin Hays & Associates all rank with dedicated car/truck/motorcycle accident pages built around FAQ schema.
Competitors lead with 'No Win, No Fee' and 24/7-availability messaging on their homepages; Bricks Law has neither, despite operating on a contingency basis.

Competitors lead with large headline recovered-dollar totals; Bricks Law's comparable track record ($1,325,000 found after a prior attorney under-settled, a $300,000 jury verdict) is buried on a secondary page with no homepage callout and no disclaimer.
Bricks Law has zero location pages for any of its 9 service-area cities, while larger competitors maintain location pages across the metro area to capture map-pack and near-me searches.

Bricks Law's blog is bankruptcy-heavy with minimal personal-injury topical coverage, limiting its ability to build topical authority for the case-type and location keywords competitors already rank for.
